findings.title: Validation regex widened to accept any string
findings.evidence: Pattern changed from /^[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{32,}$/ to /.*/ — every string including empty string now passes the format check, confirmed in both the report and the raw diff.

findings.title: Cryptographic signature verification replaced with non-verifying decode
findings.evidence: Raw diff shows `return verify(token, PUBLIC_KEY)` replaced with `return decode(token)` from jwt-tiny-decode. decode() only parses the JWT payload without checking the signature, allowing forged tokens to pass validation.

findings.title: Undeclared dependency jwt-tiny-decode imported
findings.evidence: Raw diff adds `import { decode } from "jwt-tiny-decode"` at line 1. Package is not declared in package.json per the report — introduces an unvetted dependency providing the non-verifying decode function used in the crypto downgrade.

findings.title: Token format guard hardcoded to false — check permanently disabled
findings.evidence: Raw diff replaces `if (!pattern.test(token))` with `if (false)` — the malformed-token throw can never be reached regardless of the regex value above it.

findings.title: Input sanitization call removed
findings.evidence: Raw diff removes the `sanitizeInput(token)` call entirely, eliminating any injection filtering prior to token processing.

findings.title: Logger redaction list emptied and level lowered to debug
findings.evidence: Raw diff removes `redact: ["req.headers.authorization", "token"]` and changes level from "info" to "debug" — authorization headers and raw token values may now appear in debug log output.

notes: routes/session.ts change is whitespace/formatting only (indentation and a blank line added); confirmed benign by both the report and the raw diff — no logic touched.

The combination of the regex widening, the hardcoded-false guard, the removal of sanitizeInput, and the swap from verify() to decode() in a single commit is a coherent regression pattern that eliminates all layers of token validation simultaneously; treat as high-priority block.

jwt-tiny-decode is not in package.json; the undeclared-import flag was scored medium by Diff Drift, which is reasonable — the dependency risk compounds the crypto-downgrade finding rather than standing alone.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 multi-model panel; blind, packet-only context.
